hero_product_name: the AnyWell YS16-1 PLUS electric wheelchair from the white-background reference photos
campaign_goal: Create one new universal 9:16 short ad featuring the same clearly obese older rider across three connected scenes without referencing any real identifiable customer.
target_audience: Clearly obese, heavyset, plus-size older adults, their spouses, and adult children buying mobility support for loved ones.
core_selling_points:
- steady outdoor confidence
- wide supportive seat
- calm self-operated motion
- dignified companionship outdoors
use_scenarios:
- back doorway threshold
- wooded backyard path
- open path near the tree line
visual_style: Warm, realistic, cinematic, portrait-first Western lifestyle advertising with route continuity and restrained emotional tone.
style_tone: warm, calm, credible, dignified, cinematic, never melodramatic
consistency_anchor: Match the real YS16-1 PLUS wheelchair from the white-background product photos in its normal open riding position only. Preserve the right-side joystick, wide seat feel, armrests, front caster, large drive wheel profile, tubular frame, footrest shape, and a compact top-back profile. Do not generate tall rear caregiver handles, upright rods, cane-like poles, squeeze levers, or any long parts sticking up above the backrest. If tiny integrated rear handles are unavoidable, keep them short, low, close to the backrest, and visually secondary. Do not show any rear/lower external battery pack, cable, folded form, semi-folded form, storage form, or rear-facing angle.
additional_info: Cast the rider as the same clearly obese, heavyset, plus-size silver-haired American man age 60+ across all three scenes. He must not drift toward average-sized or slightly stocky. Keep the same broad torso and shoulders, rounded belly under clothing, thicker arms and legs, heavier seated posture, clean-shaven soft face, silver hair, charcoal knit zip jacket, muted blue shirt, dark trousers, and calm body language in every shot. Keep the same wife across all scenes as a warm but non-dominant companion in a cream cardigan and muted blue top. Preserve left-to-right route direction from scene 1 through scene 3 so the ad feels like one continuous outing. Faces should stay soft and non-identifiable, never crisp portrait close-ups. Never place the camera behind the rider. The rider must visibly self-operate the wheelchair in every moving shot with his right hand on the right-side joystick. The wife must not push the chair, touch the handles, or operate the motion. This edit should also support a senior-friendly final ad: short spoken lines, large readable subtitles, and clear emphasis on product highlights and trust signals.
music: Warm restrained cinematic underscore with soft ambient texture.

concepts.title: One Continuous Route
concepts.core_theme: The same rider moves from home into nature with visible continuity and quiet confidence.
concepts.main_theme: Freedom should feel steady and close to home.
concepts.cta: See how AnyWell helps bring the outdoors back within reach.
concepts.visual_style: Soft morning-to-daylight route progression, natural greenery, realistic portrait framing, same rider and partner throughout.
concepts.continuity_rider_anchor: Keep the exact same clearly obese, heavyset, plus-size silver-haired older man and the exact same wife across all three shots. Preserve his broad torso and shoulders, rounded belly, thicker arms and legs, heavier seated posture, silver hair, clean-shaven soft face, charcoal knit jacket, muted blue shirt, dark trousers, and joystick-side orientation consistently. Keep the same compact top-back silhouette too: no tall rear poles, no caregiver handles, and no squeeze levers protruding above the backrest.
concepts.use_scenarios:
- doorway threshold
- wooded backyard path
- brighter open path near trees
concepts.additional_info: This must feel like one continuous route, not three unrelated clips. Scene 1 starts at the back doorway. Scene 2 is clearly farther along a narrower wooded path. Scene 3 opens into a brighter, less enclosed space with more sky and breathing room. Keep left-to-right motion in all three scenes. The rider must independently drive in every moving shot with the right hand using a natural thumb-and-index-finger precision pinch on the joystick knob. Keep spoken copy short and senior-friendly so subtitles can be larger and easier to read.

shots.scene_description: The same clearly obese, heavyset, plus-size silver-haired American man in his 60s sits in the AnyWell YS16-1 PLUS at a quiet back doorway threshold, visibly filling the seat with his broad torso, rounded belly, thicker arms and legs, and heavier seated posture. He wears a charcoal knit zip jacket over a muted blue shirt with dark trousers. His right hand uses a natural thumb-and-index-finger precision pinch on the right-side joystick knob as he independently begins moving left to right out of the doorway. The same wife in a cream cardigan and muted blue top follows nearby with warm relaxed body language, never touching the chair. The camera stays on the joystick-side front profile so the viewer sees his front torso, soft side profile, right forearm, joystick hand, front caster, armrest, and side wheel profile. The top-back area stays compact and unobtrusive behind him, with no tall rods, caregiver handles, or lever details rising above the backrest. The back panel and lower rear quadrant remain hidden.
shots.camera_movement: smooth joystick-side front-profile push beginning nearly still at the doorway and easing outward as the chair starts forward
shots.lighting: soft morning daylight with gentle natural spill from the doorway and clean outdoor highlights
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait medium-wide front-side composition with visible doorway threshold behind him, rider and chair large in frame, anonymous North American home details only, no readable signage
shots.transition_anchor: end with the chair fully off the threshold and continuing left to right toward the wooded path
shots.voiceover_en: He drives it himself with the right-hand joystick, and the wide 22-inch seat feels secure from the first move.
shots.key_message: Independent driving and wide-seat comfort are clear from the first shot.
shots.sfx: soft wheel roll, distant birds, light morning ambience

shots.scene_description: The exact same clearly obese, heavyset, plus-size silver-haired man continues along a narrower wooded backyard path, still moving left to right in the same wheelchair with his right hand clearly on the joystick and independently controlling the chair. His body type, clothing, posture, silver hair, clean-shaven soft face, and seat-filling silhouette remain obviously identical to scene one. The same wife walks a step to the side, keeping pace without touching the chair. The camera tracks from the joystick-side front profile so his front torso, soft facial profile, right forearm, joystick hand, armrest, and side wheel profile remain readable. The top-back area stays compact and unobtrusive behind him, with no tall rods, caregiver handles, or lever details rising above the backrest. Greenery is closer to camera and the house feels farther behind, making this clearly the middle of the route.
shots.camera_movement: steady joystick-side front-profile tracking shot with subtle forward drift and natural parallax from nearby leaves and branches
shots.lighting: filtered daylight through trees with realistic contrast and soft natural shadows
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait medium shot with shrubs and leaves closer to camera, rider and chair taking most of the frame height, spouse softly visible beside him, home distant or obscured
shots.transition_anchor: end with the chair approaching a brighter opening in the trees while still moving left to right
shots.voiceover_en: With 500 watts of smooth power and 330-pound support, the ride stays calm and easy to control.
shots.key_message: Power and steady control read clearly in the middle shot.
shots.sfx: soft gravel texture, leaves moving lightly, outdoor air

shots.scene_description: The exact same clearly obese, heavyset, plus-size silver-haired man and the same wife emerge into a brighter open path near the tree line, with more sky and open space than the previous shot. He remains clearly the same rider with the same charcoal jacket, muted blue shirt, dark trousers, silver hair, clean-shaven soft face, broad torso, rounded belly, thicker arms and legs, and heavier seat-filling posture. He continues moving left to right with his right hand visible on the joystick while the wife walks beside him at the same pace without pushing. The camera stays front-side on the joystick side, showing his chest or soft side profile, right forearm, joystick hand, and front-side wheelchair profile. The top-back area stays compact and unobtrusive behind him, with no tall rods, caregiver handles, or lever details rising above the backrest. Never show his back or the lower rear quadrant.
shots.camera_movement: gentle front-side moving settle that follows the final glide into a calm hero frame without becoming a portrait close-up
shots.lighting: clean daylight with a brighter open feel, soft skin-tone rolloff, and realistic depth
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait hero composition with more sky and open space than scene two, path and trees framing the pair, rider and chair prominent, no dense foliage crowding the frame
shots.transition_anchor: end on a calm confident hold suitable for a branded close
shots.voiceover_en: AnyWell backs it with FDA-cleared safety, a 30-day trial, easy returns, and simple PayPal financing.
shots.key_message: The close lands on trust signals in short readable phrases.
shots.sfx: light footsteps beside the chair, soft breeze, distant birds
